response:
Sec. 1  
[TO DETERMINE WHEN PUBLISHER A'S OFFER IS MORE LUCRATIVE THAN PUBLISHER B'S OFFER, WE COMPARE THE TOTAL EARNINGS FROM BOTH PUBLISHERS.]

Sec. 2  
[LET X REPRESENT THE TOTAL SALES REVENUE FROM THE BOOK, WHICH IS THE VARIABLE WE WILL USE TO COMPARE THE TWO OFFERS.]

Sec. 3  
[PUBLISHER A OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF $3,000 PLUS 5% OF THE SALES REVENUE, WHICH CAN BE EXPRESSED AS 3000 + 0.05X.]

Sec. 4  
[PUBLISHER B OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF $1,500 PLUS 10% OF THE SALES REVENUE, WHICH CAN BE EXPRESSED AS 1500 + 0.10X.]

Sec. 5  
[TO FIND THE RANGE OF SALES REVENUE WHERE PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE, WE SET UP THE INEQUALITY 3000 + 0.05X > 1500 + 0.10X.]

Sec. 6  
[SOLVING THE INEQUALITY 3000 + 0.05X > 1500 + 0.10X YIELDS THE RANGE OF SALES REVENUE WHERE PUBLISHER A IS MORE BENEFICIAL.]

Sec. 7  
[WITHIN THE ANTICIPATED RANGE OF $10,000 TO $40,000 IN SALES REVENUE, PUBLISHER A BECOMES MORE LUCRATIVE ABOVE A CERTAIN THRESHOLD.]

Sec. 8  
[THE SPECIFIC RANGE WITHIN $10,000 TO $40,000 WHERE PUBLISHER A'S OFFER IS MORE BENEFICIAL IS DETERMINED BY SOLVING THE INEQUALITY AND INTERSECTING IT WITH THE ANTICIPATED RANGE.]
